provider_contact: Annika Reinhardt, Head of Client Operations
customer_address: Level 14, 200 George Street, Sydney, NSW 2000, Australia
renewal_terms: Auto-renews for successive 12-month periods unless either party provides 90 days' written notice of non-renewal
services_covered: Managed Kubernetes hosting (Production and Staging clusters), Object storage, Managed PostgreSQL databases, CDN, and 24/7 infrastructure monitoring as specified in Service Order SO-2026-LH-003

sla_metrics.measurement_method: Synthetic monitoring from 5 global probe locations, measured at 60-second intervals
sla_metrics.penalty: Service credits per credit schedule below

sla_metrics.measurement_method: Server-side latency measured at the load balancer, excluding client network time
sla_metrics.penalty: 5% monthly service credit if exceeded for 3+ consecutive measurement periods

sla_metrics.measurement_method: Object integrity verification via checksums across replicated storage nodes
sla_metrics.penalty: Full remediation and data recovery at Provider's expense; liability per Master Agreement

sla_metrics.target: 100% of scheduled daily backups completed successfully
sla_metrics.measurement_method: Automated backup verification with integrity check and restore test sampling
sla_metrics.penalty: 2% monthly service credit per missed backup exceeding 2 in a calendar month

sla_metrics.target: Status page update within 15 minutes of confirmed Severity 1 incident
sla_metrics.measurement_method: Timestamp of first public status page update vs. incident confirmation timestamp
sla_metrics.penalty: 1% monthly service credit per occurrence exceeding 15-minute threshold

maintenance_notice: Minimum 72 hours advance notice for scheduled maintenance; 5 business days for maintenance expected to exceed 30 minutes
uptime_exclusions: Scheduled maintenance windows, force majeure events, Customer-initiated configuration changes, and third-party DNS or network provider outages outside Provider's control
escalation_procedure:
Tier 1: On-call engineer (immediate)
Tier 2: Senior infrastructure engineer (after 30 minutes without resolution)
Tier 3: Engineering Manager and Head of Client Operations (after 2 hours without resolution for Severity 1)
Tier 4: VP of Engineering and customer executive sponsor (after 4 hours without resolution for Severity 1)
All escalations logged in the shared incident management portal with real-time updates.
credit_schedule:
Monthly uptime 99.90%–99.95%: 5% service credit
Monthly uptime 99.00%–99.89%: 15% service credit
Monthly uptime 95.00%–98.99%: 25% service credit
Monthly uptime below 95.00%: 40% service credit and Customer may terminate without penalty
credit_limitations: Service credits are capped at 40% of the monthly service fees for the affected service. Credits are applied to the next billing cycle and are not redeemable for cash. Customer must request credits in writing within 30 days of the applicable monthly reporting period. Credits do not carry over beyond 6 months.
reporting_terms: Provider will deliver a monthly Service Level Report within 10 business days of each calendar month-end. Reports will include: uptime percentage, incident summary with root cause analysis for any Severity 1 or 2 incidents, latency percentile data, backup completion status, and credit calculations. Reports are accessible via the customer portal and optionally delivered by email to designated recipients.
review_terms: The parties will conduct a quarterly SLA review meeting to discuss performance trends, upcoming infrastructure changes, and potential SLA adjustments. Either party may propose amendments to this SLA with 60 days' written notice; amendments require mutual written agreement. Material service changes by Provider require SLA review within 30 days.
